versions.label: LSB
versions.text: "Again I say to you, that if two of you agree on earth about anything that they may ask, it shall be done for them by My Father who is in heaven. For where two or three have gathered together in My name, I am there in their midst."

context_lines: In **Matthew 18**, Jesus teaches His disciples about humility, stumbling, restoration, forgiveness, and life together in His kingdom. Its language about agreement, witnesses, and gathered presence belongs to this communal and disciplinary context, not to isolated proof-texting.
explanation_lines: The common assumption is that this is a prayer promise — get two believers to agree on a request, and God is obligated to grant it. The more people agreeing, the more power behind the prayer. This reading has produced entire prayer movements built around "corporate agreement" as a mechanism for guaranteed results.
The problem is that this passage is not about prayer at all. It has been completely removed from its context.
